name: St. John's Wort
benefit: Mild-to-moderate depression; SSRI-comparable efficacy in trials. Strong drug interactions.
recipe: 300 mg standardized extract (0.3% hypericin) 3× daily for 4–6 weeks.
attribution: Major CYP3A4 induction — check all medications

name: Valerian
benefit: Sedative for onset insomnia; slower-acting than OTC sleep aids.
recipe: 400–900 mg extract 30–60 min before sleep.

name: Lavender
benefit: Anxiolytic; oral Silexan formulation matched lorazepam in one trial.
recipe: 80 mg Silexan daily; or aromatherapy and tea.

name: Passionflower
benefit: Mild anxiolytic and sleep aid; GABAergic activity.
recipe: 1 tsp dried aerial parts as tea before bed; or 400 mg extract.

name: Chamomile
benefit: Gentle sedative, digestive, anti-inflammatory. Apigenin binds BZD receptors mildly.
recipe: 1 tsp flowers in hot water 5 min.

name: Lemon Balm
benefit: Anxiolytic and antiviral (HSV); rosmarinic acid and terpenes.
recipe: 1–2 tsp fresh/dried leaf as tea 2–3× daily; topical cream for cold sores.

name: Skullcap
benefit: Gentle nervine; anxiety, tension, mild tremor.
recipe: 1 tsp dried herb as tea; or tincture.

name: California Poppy
benefit: Mild sedative and analgesic; non-addictive relative of opium poppy.
recipe: 1 tsp herb as tea; or tincture.

name: Kava
benefit: Anxiolytic; effective for generalized anxiety in trials. Noble-cultivar only.
recipe: Traditional: 3–6 g noble root powder in water. Short courses.
sourcing: Pacific Island cultivars — noble only, avoid tudei.
attribution: Rare hepatotoxicity reports, mostly with non-noble cultivars

name: Rhodiola
benefit: Adaptogen; reduces fatigue and burnout symptoms in trials.
recipe: 200–400 mg standardized extract (3% rosavins) morning.

name: Holy Basil (Tulsi)
benefit: Duplicate of Tulsi.

name: Ginkgo
benefit: Circulation and cognition; mixed trials for dementia.
recipe: 120–240 mg standardized extract (EGb 761) daily.

name: Milk Thistle
benefit: Liver protection; silymarin mitigates hepatotoxicity.
recipe: 140 mg silymarin 3× daily with meals.

name: Dandelion
benefit: Liver-stimulating bitter, gentle diuretic; leaf and root differ.
recipe: Leaf as salad or tea; root decoction as coffee substitute.

name: Burdock
benefit: Blood-purifying root; inulin-rich for gut and skin support.
recipe: Fresh root like parsnip; or decoction 1 tbsp dried root.

name: Yellow Dock
benefit: Iron-rich liver herb; chronic skin conditions, constipation.
recipe: 1 tsp root decoction twice daily.

name: Nettle
benefit: Highly nutritive; iron, calcium, potassium. Antihistamine effect; BPH support.
recipe: Fresh leaves cooked as spinach; dried as tea; root for BPH.

name: Red Raspberry Leaf
benefit: Uterine tonic; traditional pregnancy tea (3rd trimester).
recipe: 1–2 cups tea daily.

name: Vitex (Chaste Tree)
benefit: PMS, PMDD, and cycle regulation; modulates prolactin.
recipe: 20–40 mg standardized extract morning.

name: Black Cohosh
benefit: Menopausal hot flashes; mixed trial results. Short-term only.
recipe: 40–80 mg extract daily; max 6 months.
attribution: Rare liver toxicity — stop if symptoms

name: Red Clover
benefit: Phytoestrogen-rich; menopause and women's health.
recipe: 1 tsp flowers as tea 2–3× daily.

name: Evening Primrose
benefit: GLA-rich seed oil; PMS breast tenderness, eczema support.
recipe: 500 mg oil 2–4× daily.

name: Borage
benefit: Higher-GLA source than evening primrose; similar uses.
recipe: 1000 mg seed oil daily.
attribution: Pyrrolizidine alkaloids — choose PA-free processed oils

name: Dong Quai (Dang Gui)
benefit: Duplicate of Dang Gui.

name: Saw Palmetto
benefit: Benign prostatic hyperplasia; mixed trial results.
recipe: 320 mg standardized extract daily.

name: Pygeum
benefit: African herbal for BPH; modest urinary improvement.
recipe: 100–200 mg standardized bark extract daily.

name: Nettle Root
benefit: BPH-specific use; combines well with saw palmetto.
recipe: 240 mg extract 2× daily.

name: Tribulus
benefit: Traditional male vitality herb; libido effect shown, testosterone effect weak.
recipe: 500 mg standardized extract 2× daily.

name: Horny Goat Weed
benefit: Traditional Chinese kidney-yang tonic; icariin may support erectile function.
recipe: 1 g leaf extract 2× daily.

name: Fenugreek (Men's)
benefit: Some studies show modest free-testosterone increase in middle-aged men.
recipe: 600 mg standardized extract daily.

name: Gotu Kola
benefit: Duplicate of Gotu Kola.

name: Calendula
benefit: Topical wound healing, anti-inflammatory; diaper rash, radiation dermatitis.
recipe: Ointment or oil applied to affected skin.

name: Comfrey
benefit: 'Knitbone' — topical healing of bruises, sprains, small fractures.
recipe: Ointment on intact skin only; short courses.
attribution: PAs hepatotoxic — external use only, intact skin

name: Arnica
benefit: Topical bruise, muscle soreness, sprain.
recipe: Gel or ointment on unbroken skin.
sourcing: Alpine; most commercial is A. chamissonis.

name: Witch Hazel
benefit: Astringent; hemorrhoids, minor skin irritation, varicose veins.
recipe: Topical distillate or bark decoction.

name: Chickweed
benefit: Cooling demulcent; topical itch and eczema, internal cooling.
recipe: Fresh salad; or topical cream.

name: Plantain
benefit: Wound-drawing, astringent, and demulcent; insect bites, coughs.
recipe: Fresh leaf mashed on bites/splinters; leaf infusion for coughs.

name: Yarrow
benefit: Styptic (stops bleeding), fever-sweating herb, menstrual regulator.
recipe: Fresh leaf on cuts; tea for fever.

name: Mullein
benefit: Demulcent expectorant for dry coughs; leaf tea or infused oil for earaches.
recipe: 1 tsp dried leaf in tea (strain well — hairs); garlic-mullein ear oil.

name: Marshmallow Root
benefit: Mucilaginous demulcent; gastritis, sore throat, cough.
recipe: Cold-water infusion 4 hr; 1 tsp root in water.

name: Slippery Elm
benefit: Demulcent for gut and throat; IBS, reflux, sore throat.
recipe: 1 tsp powder in warm water as paste.
sourcing: Eastern North America; endangered — buy sustainable.

name: Thyme
benefit: Antimicrobial expectorant; coughs, mild infections.
recipe: 1 tsp fresh/dried as tea 3× daily during cold.

name: Sage
benefit: Sore throats, excessive sweating (menopausal/hyperhidrosis), memory.
recipe: 1 tsp dried leaf as tea or gargle.

name: Rosemary
benefit: Memory, circulation, digestive tonic.
recipe: Fresh culinary use; or 1 tsp dried leaf as tea.

name: Oregano
benefit: Antimicrobial (carvacrol-rich); oil for gut and respiratory infections.
recipe: 100–300 mg oregano oil capsules short-term; or culinary use.

name: Peppermint
benefit: IBS relief (enteric-coated oil); headaches; digestion.
recipe: Enteric-coated 0.2 ml oil 3× daily for IBS; or tea after meals.

name: Fennel
benefit: Carminative, mild lactagogue, anise-flavored.
recipe: 1 tsp seeds chewed after meals; or tea.

name: Caraway
benefit: Classical carminative; IBS and colic.
recipe: Seeds chewed after meals; or with peppermint oil.

name: Dill
benefit: Infant colic (dill water), mild digestive.
recipe: Seed infusion; traditional gripe water.

name: Anise
benefit: Coughs, digestion, lactation.
recipe: 1 tsp seeds in tea 2–3× daily.

name: Angelica
benefit: European digestive bitter; warming, carminative.
recipe: Root tincture or tea, small doses.

name: Gentian
benefit: Intensely bitter digestive aperitif; stimulates gastric secretion.
recipe: Small amounts tincture before meals.

name: Artichoke Leaf
benefit: Bile stimulation; cholesterol, dyspepsia.
recipe: 320–640 mg standardized extract daily.

name: Bitters blend
benefit: Digestive bitters (angelica, gentian, citrus peel) stimulate upstream digestion.
recipe: 1 dropperful tincture in water 15 min before meals.

name: Hawthorn
benefit: Cardiac tonic; mild heart failure (NYHA I-II) support.
recipe: 160–900 mg standardized extract daily.
sourcing: European tree; leaves, flowers, and berries.

name: Motherwort
benefit: Cardiovascular calmative; palpitations with anxiety.
recipe: Tincture or tea 1–3× daily.

name: Olive Leaf
benefit: Blood-pressure support; cardiovascular and antiviral.
recipe: 500 mg standardized extract (oleuropein) daily.

name: Garlic
benefit: Duplicate of Garlic main entry.

name: Onion
benefit: Quercetin-rich; cardiovascular support, mild antiviral.
recipe: Raw or cooked daily; traditional onion-honey cough syrup.

name: Horseradish
benefit: Circulatory stimulant, decongestant, urinary antiseptic.
recipe: Fresh grated root in small amounts.

name: Mustard
benefit: Topical warming plaster for chest congestion.
recipe: Classical mustard plaster on chest — test skin first.

name: Capsaicin / Chili
benefit: Topical pain relief (neuropathic); circulatory stimulant.
recipe: 0.025–0.075% topical cream 3–4× daily.

name: Black Pepper
benefit: Warming digestive; piperine increases absorption of curcumin and other compounds.
recipe: Freshly ground with meals, especially turmeric.

name: Cinnamon
benefit: Blood-sugar support; use Ceylon not Cassia for long-term (coumarin levels).
recipe: ½–1 tsp Ceylon cinnamon daily in food.

name: Cloves
benefit: Potent antimicrobial (eugenol); toothache, digestion.
recipe: Whole clove held by aching tooth; clove oil drop for localized relief.

name: Cardamom
benefit: Digestive and respiratory warming spice.
recipe: 3–5 pods crushed in tea.

name: Nutmeg
benefit: Mild sedative in small doses; toxic and hallucinogenic in excess.
recipe: Pinch only, grated fresh; in warm milk before bed.
attribution: Doses above 5 g can be toxic

name: Saffron
benefit: Mild depression and PMS support; clinical trials promising.
recipe: 15 mg standardized extract 2× daily; or culinary.

name: Green Tea
benefit: EGCG-rich antioxidant; cardiovascular, metabolic, cognitive support.
recipe: 2–4 cups daily; or 500 mg EGCG extract with food.

name: Black Tea
benefit: Theaflavins; cardiovascular and cognitive support.
recipe: 2–4 cups daily.

name: Rooibos
benefit: South African caffeine-free; antioxidant and calming.
recipe: 1 tsp in hot water; unlimited.

name: Honeybush
benefit: South African honey-scented tea; phytoestrogen-mild.
recipe: 1 tsp in hot water.

name: Devil's Claw
benefit: Joint pain; osteoarthritis trials show modest benefit.
recipe: 2–4 g dried root as decoction; or 600 mg extract 3× daily.

name: White Willow
benefit: Salicin — precursor to aspirin; back pain, osteoarthritis.
recipe: 240 mg salicin daily; avoid with aspirin allergy.

name: Meadowsweet
benefit: Another salicylate source; gentler on stomach, digestive anti-inflammatory.
recipe: 1 tsp flowers/leaves as tea.

name: Feverfew
benefit: Migraine prophylaxis; reduces frequency in trials.
recipe: 100–150 mg standardized extract daily for 2–3 months.

name: Butterbur
benefit: Migraine and allergic rhinitis; PA-free products only.
recipe: 75 mg PA-free extract 2× daily.

name: Bilberry
benefit: Anthocyanin-rich; eye health, vascular support.
recipe: 160 mg extract 2× daily.

name: Cranberry
benefit: Recurrent UTI prevention; proanthocyanidins.
recipe: 36 mg PAC standardized daily; or 250 ml juice.

name: Elder Flower
benefit: Diaphoretic; early-stage cold with fever.
recipe: 1 tsp flowers in hot water 2–3× daily.

name: Linden / Lime Flower
benefit: Calming diaphoretic; colds with anxiety or children.
recipe: 1 tsp flowers in hot water.

name: Horehound
benefit: Expectorant for wet cough; traditional in cough lozenges.
recipe: 1 tsp herb as tea or lozenges.

name: Eucalyptus
benefit: Decongestant; steam inhalation, chest rubs.
recipe: 5 drops oil in hot water for steam; or diluted topical.

name: Cleavers
benefit: Lymphatic drainage; gentle diuretic for skin conditions.
recipe: Fresh herb as tea or juice; or tincture.

name: Chickweed
benefit: Cooling demulcent for skin irritation; nutritive spring green.
recipe: Fresh leaves in salads; poultice for skin.

name: Fig
benefit: Blood sugar support; mild laxative fruit and leaf.
recipe: Fresh fruit; leaf tea for blood sugar.

name: Rosehip
benefit: High vitamin C; joint support and immune tonic.
recipe: Dried hips as tea or syrup.

name: Turkey Tail
benefit: Immune modulating mushroom; PSK and PSP polysaccharides.
recipe: Decoction 2–3 g dried mushroom daily.

name: Chaga
benefit: Antioxidant-rich adaptogen; traditional Siberian tonic.
recipe: Decoction or tincture of sclerotium.

name: Clove
benefit: Antimicrobial spice; dental pain and digestive support.
recipe: Whole clove for toothache; 1–2 drops oil diluted.

name: Tea Tree
benefit: Topical antimicrobial; acne, fungal infections.
recipe: 5% dilution topically; undiluted for spot treatment.

name: Goldenseal
benefit: Berberine-rich antimicrobial; short-term use for infection.
recipe: 250 mg root 3× daily for 7–10 days.

name: Oregon Grape
benefit: Berberine substitute for goldenseal; skin and GI infections.
recipe: Root decoction or tincture.

name: Barberry
benefit: Berberine source; metabolic, GI.
recipe: Root or bark decoction; or 500 mg berberine 2–3× daily.

name: Berberine (isolated)
benefit: Isolated alkaloid; blood sugar and cholesterol — comparable to metformin in small trials.
recipe: 500 mg 3× daily with meals.
sourcing: Capsule form from Coptis, Berberis, Hydrastis.
attribution: Recent clinical research supports metabolic effects

name: Ivy Leaf
benefit: Pediatric cough syrup; Prospan extract widely prescribed in Europe.
recipe: 5–7.5 ml syrup 2–3× daily.

name: Agrimony
benefit: Astringent; diarrhea, mild cystitis.
recipe: 1 tsp herb as tea.

name: Hyssop
benefit: Expectorant; classical biblical herb.
recipe: 1 tsp herb as tea.

name: Rose
benefit: Heart-soothing; rose-water, rose petal tea for grief and skin.
recipe: Petal tea; hydrosol on skin.

name: Hibiscus
benefit: Blood pressure reduction; sour cooling tea.
recipe: 1 tbsp flowers in hot water 5 min, 2–3× daily.

name: Cordyceps
benefit: Duplicate of TCM entry.

name: Astragalus
benefit: Duplicate of Huang Qi.

name: Schisandra
benefit: Duplicate of Wu Wei Zi.

name: Rhaponticum (Maral Root)
benefit: Siberian adaptogen; physical stamina, traditional in athletes.
recipe: Tincture or 500 mg extract.

name: Spirulina
benefit: Protein-rich cyanobacteria; iron, B12 analog (limited bioavailability).
recipe: 1–5 g daily in smoothies.

name: Chlorella
benefit: Green algae; traditional detox (heavy metals evidence preliminary).
recipe: 2–5 g daily broken-cell wall powder.

name: Spearmint
benefit: Anti-androgenic (PCOS hirsutism); digestive.
recipe: 2 cups tea daily for PCOS hirsutism.

name: Damiana
benefit: Mexican traditional aphrodisiac and mild antidepressant.
recipe: 1 tsp leaves as tea.

name: Muira Puama
benefit: Amazonian 'potency wood' — traditional sexual tonic.
recipe: Bark tincture or decoction.

name: Catuaba
benefit: Brazilian aphrodisiac and nervine.
recipe: Bark tincture 1–2 ml 2× daily.

name: Yohimbe
benefit: African tree bark; yohimbine alkaloid used for erectile dysfunction.
recipe: Prescription product (yohimbine HCl) preferable — crude bark unreliable.
attribution: BP and heart effects; don't combine with MAOIs

name: Kratom
benefit: Southeast Asian leaf; opioid-receptor agonist, pain and withdrawal. Regulated.

name: Blue Lotus
benefit: Ancient Egyptian mildly psychoactive flower; calming.
recipe: Wine infusion or tea from flowers.

name: Mugwort
benefit: Dream-enhancing, digestive bitter, menstrual regulator.
recipe: 1 tsp herb as tea; or smudge.
attribution: Thujone content — avoid long-term and in pregnancy

name: Wormwood
benefit: Bitter tonic and traditional antiparasitic; base of absinthe.
recipe: Small doses in formulas; short-term only.

name: Artemisia annua
benefit: Source of artemisinin antimalarial (Nobel 2015). Research in other conditions ongoing.
recipe: Artemisinin-derived drugs used per protocol; crude herb unreliable.
attribution: WHO recommends ACT therapy for malaria

name: Neem
benefit: Duplicate of Neem.

name: Tongkat Ali
benefit: Southeast Asian tonic; small studies suggest testosterone and stress effects.
recipe: 200–400 mg standardized extract daily.

name: Kudzu
benefit: Alcohol craving reduction in small trials; also for neck tension.
recipe: 300 mg root extract 2× daily before drinking.

name: Pine Bark
benefit: OPC-rich; circulation, cognitive, ADHD in children.
recipe: 50–150 mg Pycnogenol daily.

name: Grape Seed Extract
benefit: OPC-rich like pine bark; vascular support.
recipe: 150–300 mg standardized extract daily.

name: Resveratrol
benefit: Polyphenol studied for longevity and cardiovascular; human data mixed.
recipe: 250–500 mg daily; often with food.

name: Olive Oil (EVOO)
benefit: Cornerstone of Mediterranean diet; polyphenol-rich extra-virgin cardiovascular.
recipe: 2–4 tbsp daily replacing other fats.

name: Flaxseed
benefit: ALA omega-3, lignans; hormone balance, cardiovascular.
recipe: 1–2 tbsp freshly ground daily.

name: Chia Seed
benefit: Omega-3, fiber, mineral-rich; Aztec staple.
recipe: 1–2 tbsp soaked in water or smoothie.

name: Hemp Seed
benefit: Complete protein and omega-3:6 balance; non-psychoactive.
recipe: 2–3 tbsp hulled seeds daily.

name: Nigella / Black Seed
benefit: Middle Eastern traditional tonic; trials for asthma, blood sugar, lipids.
recipe: 1–3 g seeds or 500 mg oil daily.

name: Propolis
benefit: Antimicrobial; cold sores, wound healing, sore throat.
recipe: Tincture spray for throat; topical for cold sores.

name: Raw Honey
benefit: Antimicrobial (Manuka particularly); cough suppressant in children over 1 year.
recipe: 1 tsp for cough; topical on wounds.

name: Royal Jelly
benefit: 10-HDA and proteins; traditional tonic, menopausal symptom research.
recipe: 500–1000 mg fresh royal jelly daily.

name: Bee Pollen
benefit: Protein, vitamins, carotenoids; tonic.
recipe: 1 tsp daily, building from small pinch for allergy testing.

name: Spilanthes
benefit: 'Toothache plant' — analgesic and antimicrobial, fresh flower buds numb the mouth.
recipe: Fresh flower bud chewed for local numbing.

name: Bladderwrack
benefit: Iodine-rich seaweed; thyroid support. Verify needs first.
recipe: 1–3 capsules daily.

name: Irish Moss
benefit: Mucilaginous seaweed; respiratory, gut, mineral-rich.
recipe: Soaked and blended into gel.

name: Colloidal Oatmeal
benefit: Topical anti-itch; eczema, dermatitis.
recipe: Oatmeal bath 1 cup ground oats in bath.

name: Oat Straw / Milky Oat
benefit: Nervine tonic; chronic stress, nicotine withdrawal.
recipe: 1 tsp herb as tea 2–3× daily; or milky-oat tincture fresh.
sourcing: Harvested in milky stage for medicinal use.
